edition_notes: v3 active: primary source for AnchorVocab.load_anchor_vocab() in qa_v3_filter. Every canonical term_id plus its synonyms becomes an anchor the rec/paragraph survival filter can match against.
v3 active: injected into both LLM prompts via _build_synonym_appendix so parser and verifier see the same closed clinical vocabulary.
v3 expansion commit d4f7b3b: grew from 157 to 237 canonical terms — added 80 entries covering IVT/EVT/BP trials, drugs (DOACs + reversal agents), devices, imaging findings, and vessel subsegments.
v3 namespace: sole live copy lives at agents/qa_v3/references/.
metadata.purpose: Clinical synonym and abbreviation dictionary for deterministic term expansion. Maps abbreviations, acronyms, and informal terms to their canonical forms and primary guideline sections. Used by both the LLM (in prompt) and Python (for keyword expansion before section routing).
metadata.source: Extracted from AIS_Data_Dictionary_Terms_Acronyms.xlsx (other Claude session) plus clinical synonym mappings.
metadata.usage: When a clinician says 'TNK', expand to 'tenecteplase' and know it lives in 4.6.2. When they say 'sICH', expand to 'symptomatic intracerebral hemorrhage' and route to 4.6.1.
v2_changes: Rebuilt category_index from actual terms content. v1 had 12 categories in terms that were missing from category_index (clinical_cardiac, core_term, guideline_framework, imaging_finding, imaging_physiology, organization, quality_program, reference_standard, risk_score, route_of_administration, study_design, systems_process) and 2 categories in category_index with no matching terms (imaging_concept, surgery).
Added reverse_index (full_term -> [term_ids]) for O(1) canonical-form -> abbreviation lookup
Added overload_table for abbreviations with alternate medical meanings, so the QAQueryParsingAgent can flag ambiguity
Added duplicate_full_terms_report so the reviewer can decide whether DTAS/DTAS_procedure and GTN/GTN_drug style splits should be merged or kept distinct

Applied bug_fixes[] (see top-level for details) — removed ICH↔HT synonym collision, removed stent-retriever brand names from EVT, removed self-duplicate synonyms (BP, DOAC, ICP, MSU, SpO2, PC-ASPECTS)
Consolidated category clinical_cardiac -> clinical_condition and outcome_scale -> outcome_measure; original v1 category preserved in _v1_category on each affected entry
changes_needing_human_review: Review the overload_table entries and decide whether to add disambiguation prompts to the LLM parser
Review duplicate_full_terms_report and decide whether to merge pairs like DTAS/DTAS_procedure, GTN/GTN_drug
Expand term coverage beyond the current 163 terms — stroke specialty reasonably needs several hundred more terms for edge cases
Review near-duplicate category taxonomy (assessment_scale vs screening_scale, clinical_finding vs clinical_condition) and document the rule or consolidate
Add section mapping for terms currently marked 'all' — some could be narrowed

bug_fixes.reason: Synonym entry was identical to the full_term, creating a noise collision.
Synonym entry was identical to the full_term, creating a noise collision.
Solitaire and Trevo are specific stent-retriever device brands, not EVT procedure synonyms. They remain on the stent_retriever entry.
HT is a separate term. HT's own clinical_context explicitly states it is distinct from ICH. Leaving this synonym on ICH caused the same string to collide with two different canonical concepts.
Synonym entry was identical to the full_term, creating a noise collision.
Synonym entry was identical to the full_term, creating a noise collision.
Synonym entry was identical to the full_term, creating a noise collision.
Synonym entry was identical to the full_term, creating a noise collision.

v1_inline_comments._comment_trials: ── Key clinical trial names (LLM confuses with English words) ──
v1_inline_comments._comment_uncommon_drugs: ── Uncommon drugs/agents the LLM may not recognize in AIS context ──
v1_inline_comments._comment_procedures: ── Procedures and techniques the LLM may misinterpret ──
v1_inline_comments._comment_imaging_concepts: ── Imaging concepts that need clinical context ──
v1_inline_comments._comment_clinical_concepts: ── Clinical concepts/scenarios the LLM needs context for ──

ABCD2.full_term: Age, Blood pressure, Clinical features, Duration, Diabetes

ASPECTS.full_term: Alberta Stroke Program Early CT Score

DOAC.clinical_context: Covers apixaban, rivaroxaban, dabigatran, edoxaban. Affects IVT eligibility.

M1.full_term: M1 segment of middle cerebral artery

M2.full_term: M2 segment of middle cerebral artery

NIHSS.full_term: National Institutes of Health Stroke Scale

AUC.full_term: area under the receiver operating characteristic curve

CRAO.clinical_context: IVT eligibility for acute visual loss, not a stroke subtype — lives in §4.6.5 Other Specific Circumstances

CT.clinical_context: Base modality — see also CTA, CTP, NCCT for specific CT types

DIDO.clinical_context: Time from arrival at first hospital to departure for transfer to higher-level center

DSM.full_term: Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders

DTAS.full_term: direct triage to the angiography suite
DTAS.clinical_context: Bypassing CT/ER to take EVT-eligible patients directly to angio suite — a workflow optimization, not an imaging modality

GPC.clinical_context: VTE prophylaxis — not beneficial for stroke patients per guideline (COR 3: No Benefit)

GTN.clinical_context: Prehospital BP lowering — guideline says NOT beneficial and potentially harmful (COR 3: No Benefit)

HBO.clinical_context: Treatment for arterial air embolism causing stroke — NOT a neuroprotective agent. Lives in §4.1 General Supportive Care, not §4.11 Neuroprotection

HT.clinical_context: Post-treatment bleeding complication — distinct from ICH (primary intracerebral hemorrhage) and sICH (symptomatic ICH)

IV.clinical_context: Route of administration — see IVT for intravenous thrombolysis specifically

MFV.clinical_context: Measured by transcranial Doppler (TCD) for cerebral blood flow monitoring

NINDS.full_term: National Institute of Neurological Disorders and Stroke
NINDS.clinical_context: Also a sICH definition used in trials (NINDS definition of sICH)

PES.clinical_context: Dysphagia treatment — reduces severity and aspiration risk (COR 2a)

RIC.clinical_context: Prehospital neuroprotective intervention using BP cuff inflation — evidence uncertain. §2.3 for prehospital use, §4.11 for neuroprotection context

RWI.clinical_context: Conflict of interest disclosures — not clinically relevant for Q&A

SSRI.clinical_context: NOT effective for motor recovery (COR 3: Harm in §5.7). Used for poststroke depression (§5.5)

DAWN.full_term: DWI or CTP Assessment with Clinical Mismatch in the Triage of Wake-Up and Late Presenting Strokes Undergoing Neurointervention with Trevo
DAWN.clinical_context: Landmark EVT trial for 6-24h window with perfusion mismatch. NOT the English word 'dawn'

DEFUSE.full_term: Diffusion and Perfusion Imaging Evaluation for Understanding Stroke Evolution
DEFUSE.clinical_context: EVT trial for 6-16h window with perfusion mismatch

EXTEND.full_term: Extending the Time for Thrombolysis in Emergency Neurological Deficits
EXTEND.clinical_context: IVT trial for extended time window (4.5-9h). NOT the English word 'extend'

HERMES.full_term: Highly Effective Reperfusion evaluated in Multiple Endovascular Stroke Trials
HERMES.clinical_context: Meta-analysis of 5 major EVT trials establishing EVT efficacy

BAOCHE.full_term: Basilar Artery Occlusion Chinese Endovascular Trial

RESIST.full_term: Remote Ischemic Conditioning in Patients With Acute Stroke Trial

WAKE-UP.full_term: Efficacy and Safety of MRI-Based Thrombolysis in Wake-Up Stroke
WAKE-UP.clinical_context: IVT for unknown-onset stroke using DWI-FLAIR mismatch. NOT the phrase 'wake up'

ESCAPE.full_term: Endovascular Treatment for Small Core and Anterior Circulation Proximal Occlusion with Emphasis on Minimizing CT to Recanalization Times
ESCAPE.clinical_context: Landmark EVT trial. NOT the English word 'escape'

SWIFT.full_term: Solitaire With the Intention for Thrombectomy
SWIFT.clinical_context: EVT device trial. NOT the English word 'swift'

REVASCAT.full_term: Randomized Trial of Revascularization with Solitaire FR Device vs Best Medical Therapy in the Treatment of Acute Stroke Due to Anterior Circulation Large Vessel Occlusion

MR CLEAN.full_term: Multicenter Randomized Clinical Trial of Endovascular Treatment for Acute Ischemic Stroke in the Netherlands
MR CLEAN.clinical_context: First positive EVT RCT (2015). NOT 'Mr. Clean'

THRACE.full_term: Mechanical Thrombectomy After Intravenous Alteplase vs Alteplase Alone After Stroke

SELECT.full_term: SELECT2 — Endovascular Therapy for Acute Ischemic Stroke With Large Ischemic Core
SELECT.clinical_context: Large-core EVT trial. NOT the English word 'select'

ATTENTION.full_term: Endovascular Treatment for Acute Basilar Artery Occlusion
ATTENTION.clinical_context: Basilar occlusion EVT, Chinese multicenter RCT.

ECASS.clinical_context: IVT time window trials. Also a sICH definition (ECASS definition)

ENCHANTED.full_term: Enhanced Control of Hypertension and Thrombolysis Stroke Study
ENCHANTED.clinical_context: Tenecteplase dose trial. NOT the English word 'enchanted'

CHANCE.full_term: Clopidogrel in High-Risk Patients with Acute Non-disabling Cerebrovascular Events
CHANCE.clinical_context: DAPT trial for minor stroke/TIA. NOT the English word 'chance'

THALES.full_term: Acute Stroke or Transient Ischaemic Attack Treated with Ticagrelor and Aspirin for Prevention of Stroke and Death
THALES.clinical_context: Ticagrelor + aspirin trial for minor stroke

DESTINY.full_term: Decompressive Surgery for the Treatment of Malignant Infarction of the Middle Cerebral Artery
DESTINY.clinical_context: Hemicraniectomy trial. NOT the English word 'destiny'

HAMLET.full_term: Hemicraniectomy After Middle Cerebral Artery Infarction with Life-Threatening Edema Trial
HAMLET.clinical_context: Hemicraniectomy trial. NOT the Shakespeare play

DECIMAL.full_term: Decompressive Craniectomy in Malignant Middle Cerebral Artery Infarcts
DECIMAL.clinical_context: Hemicraniectomy trial. NOT the number system

FOOD.clinical_context: Dysphagia/nutrition trial. NOT the English word 'food'

CLOTS.full_term: Clots in Legs Or sTockings after Stroke
CLOTS.clinical_context: VTE prophylaxis trial (GPC). NOT the English word 'clots'

COMPASS.clinical_context: Direct aspiration vs stent retriever first-line.

POINT.full_term: Platelet-Oriented Inhibition in New TIA and Minor Ischemic Stroke
POINT.clinical_context: DAPT trial for TIA/minor stroke. NOT the English word 'point'

prourokinase.clinical_context: Intra-arterial thrombolytic used in PROACT II trial. Historical — not standard of care. Different from IV tPA/TNK

reteplase.clinical_context: Alternative thrombolytic mentioned in guideline. Not FDA-approved for stroke. Different from alteplase and tenecteplase

streptokinase.clinical_context: Older thrombolytic — guideline explicitly says NOT recommended for AIS (COR 3: Harm)

desmoteplase.clinical_context: Failed in clinical trials (DIAS, DEDAS). NOT recommended

nerinetide.clinical_context: PSD-95 inhibitor tested in ESCAPE-NA1 trial. Neuroprotective agent, section 4.11

glibenclamide.clinical_context: SUR1-TRPM4 channel inhibitor for malignant edema. NOT a diabetes drug in this context — used to reduce cerebral edema after large stroke

GTN_drug.clinical_context: See GTN entry. Transdermal nitroglycerin for prehospital BP management — NOT beneficial per guideline

decompressive craniectomy.full_term: decompressive craniectomy (surgical procedure for malignant edema)
decompressive craniectomy.clinical_context: Removal of skull bone to relieve pressure from malignant cerebral edema. Lives in §6 Complications, not §4 Acute Treatment

hemicraniectomy.clinical_context: Specific type of decompressive craniectomy — removal of half the skull. Studied in DESTINY, HAMLET, DECIMAL trials

ventriculostomy.clinical_context: Drainage of CSF for obstructive hydrocephalus after cerebellar stroke

tandem occlusion.clinical_context: Simultaneous extracranial ICA and intracranial artery occlusion. Requires combined EVT approach. NOT two separate occlusions

stent retriever.clinical_context: Device deployed into clot, expands, and retrieves clot during EVT. One of two main EVT techniques (vs aspiration)

contact aspiration.clinical_context: Suction-based clot removal during EVT. Alternative to stent retriever technique

DTAS_procedure.full_term: direct triage to the angiography suite
DTAS_procedure.clinical_context: See DTAS entry. Workflow bypassing ER/CT to bring EVT patients straight to angio

Tmax.full_term: time to maximum of residue function
Tmax.clinical_context: Perfusion parameter measuring delay in blood arrival. Tmax >6s defines ischemic penumbra (tissue at risk). Key threshold for EVT eligibility in DAWN/DEFUSE trials

ischemic core.clinical_context: Brain tissue already dead — measured by DWI (MRI) or rCBF <30% (CTP). Core volume determines EVT eligibility: <70mL in DAWN, <50mL in SELECT2

ischemic penumbra.clinical_context: Brain tissue that is ischemic but still viable — the target of reperfusion therapy. Defined as Tmax >6s minus core. NOT the Penumbra device brand

DWI-FLAIR mismatch.full_term: DWI-FLAIR mismatch (imaging sign for unknown onset stroke)
DWI-FLAIR mismatch.clinical_context: DWI positive but FLAIR negative suggests stroke onset <4.5 hours ago. Used to select unknown-onset patients for IVT (WAKE-UP trial)

target mismatch.clinical_context: Ratio of penumbra to core volume that predicts benefit from EVT. DEFUSE criteria: mismatch ratio ≥1.8, ischemic core <70mL, mismatch volume ≥15mL

wake-up stroke.full_term: wake-up stroke (stroke with unknown time of onset)
wake-up stroke.clinical_context: Patient wakes up with stroke symptoms — onset time unknown. Eligible for IVT if DWI-FLAIR mismatch (WAKE-UP trial) or perfusion mismatch (EXTEND)

stroke mimic.full_term: stroke mimic (non-stroke condition presenting like stroke)
stroke mimic.clinical_context: Conditions that look like stroke but aren't: seizure, migraine, hypoglycemia, etc. IVT is relatively safe in mimics (low sICH risk)

malignant edema.clinical_context: Severe brain swelling after large MCA stroke — 80% mortality without surgery. Treated with decompressive craniectomy (§6.2) and possibly glibenclamide (§6.1)

door-to-groin.clinical_context: Time from hospital arrival to femoral artery puncture for EVT. Target <60-90 minutes. Different from DTN (door-to-needle for IVT)

ECASS-III.full_term: European Cooperative Acute Stroke Study III
ECASS-III.clinical_context: Established IV alteplase efficacy in the 3-4.5h window.

EPITHET.clinical_context: Perfusion-diffusion mismatch selection for extended-window alteplase.

EXTEND-IA TNK.full_term: Tenecteplase versus Alteplase before Endovascular Therapy for Ischemic Stroke
EXTEND-IA TNK.clinical_context: Tenecteplase 0.25 mg/kg superior to alteplase for reperfusion before EVT.

THAWS.full_term: Thrombolysis for Acute Wake-up and Unclear Onset Strokes with Alteplase at 0.6 mg/kg
THAWS.clinical_context: Japanese trial of 0.6 mg/kg alteplase for wake-up stroke selected by DWI-FLAIR mismatch.

MR WITNESS.clinical_context: Safety of IV alteplase in unwitnessed-onset stroke with favorable DWI-FLAIR imaging.

AcT.clinical_context: Canadian non-inferiority trial — tenecteplase 0.25 mg/kg non-inferior to alteplase.

ATTEST.full_term: Alteplase-Tenecteplase Trial Evaluation for Stroke Thrombolysis

TASTE.full_term: Tenecteplase versus Alteplase for Stroke Thrombolysis Evaluation

TIMELESS.full_term: Tenecteplase in Stroke Patients Between 4.5 and 24 Hours
TIMELESS.clinical_context: Tenecteplase in extended window with perfusion imaging selection.

TWIST.full_term: Tenecteplase in Wake-up Ischemic Stroke Trial

PRISMS.full_term: Potential of rtPA for Ischemic Strokes with Mild Symptoms
PRISMS.clinical_context: Alteplase in minor stroke — trial stopped early, no benefit.

MaRISS.full_term: Mild and Rapidly Improving Stroke Study
MaRISS.clinical_context: Registry of mild or rapidly-improving stroke outcomes with and without IVT.

RAISE.clinical_context: Reteplase superior to alteplase in Chinese trial.

MOST.clinical_context: Adjunctive argatroban/eptifibatide with IV thrombolysis — no benefit.

NOR-SASS.full_term: Norwegian Sonothrombolysis in Acute Stroke Study

CLOTBUST-ER.full_term: Combined Lysis of Thrombus in Brain Ischemia Using Transcranial Ultrasound and Systemic tPA — Enhanced Regimen

DEFUSE-3.full_term: Endovascular Therapy Following Imaging Evaluation for Ischemic Stroke 3

DEFUSE-2.full_term: Diffusion-Weighted Imaging Evaluation for Understanding Stroke Evolution 2

SELECT2.full_term: Randomized Controlled Trial of EVT vs Medical Management for Large Ischemic Stroke 2
SELECT2.clinical_context: Large-core EVT (ASPECTS 3-5, core volume 50-100 mL) — benefit confirmed.

ANGEL-ASPECT.full_term: Evaluation of Endovascular Therapy for Large-Core Ischemic Stroke in China

RESCUE-Japan LIMIT.full_term: Recovery by Endovascular Salvage for Cerebral Ultra-Acute Embolism Japan Large Ischemic Core

TENSION.full_term: Efficacy and Safety of Thrombectomy in Stroke with extensive Ischemic Injury

BEST.full_term: Endovascular Treatment for Basilar Artery Occlusion Stroke Trial

SKIP.full_term: Skip Intravenous tPA in Endovascular Therapy

DIRECT-MT.full_term: Direct Intraarterial Thrombectomy in Order to Revascularize AIS Patients

DEVT.full_term: Direct Endovascular Thrombectomy vs Bridging Therapy

DIRECT-SAFE.full_term: Direct Thrombectomy vs Bridging Thrombolysis with Thrombectomy

ASTER.full_term: Contact Aspiration versus Stent Retriever for Successful Revascularization

GOLIATH.full_term: General Anesthesia vs Conscious Sedation for Endovascular Treatment

SIESTA.full_term: Sedation vs Intubation for Endovascular Stroke Treatment

ENCHANTED-2.full_term: Enhanced Control of Hypertension and Thrombolysis Stroke Study 2
ENCHANTED-2.clinical_context: Intensive BP lowering after EVT — worse outcomes; post-EVT SBP <140 harmful.

OPTIMAL-BP.full_term: Optimal Blood Pressure Management for Endovascular Treatment

BP-TARGET.full_term: Blood Pressure Target in Acute Stroke to Reduce Hemorrhage after Endovascular Therapy

RIGHT-2.full_term: Prehospital Transdermal Glyceryl Trinitrate in Patients with Ultra-Acute Stroke

Metalyse_merged_into_TNK._note: Merged into TNK entry — Metalyse is a brand name for tenecteplase (TNK). Kept as tombstone for audit trail.

argatroban.clinical_context: Direct thrombin inhibitor studied as IVT adjunct (MOST).

cangrelor.clinical_context: IV P2Y12 inhibitor used peri-EVT with stent placement.

eptifibatide.clinical_context: Glycoprotein IIb/IIIa inhibitor studied as IVT adjunct (MOST).

dabigatran.clinical_context: DOAC — relevant to IVT contraindication checklist.

andexanet.clinical_context: Factor Xa inhibitor reversal agent (apixaban, rivaroxaban).

icatibant.clinical_context: Bradykinin B2 receptor antagonist — treats alteplase-induced orolingual angioedema.

tranexamic acid.clinical_context: Antifibrinolytic used in sICH reversal algorithm (Table 5).

cryoprecipitate.clinical_context: Fibrinogen replacement in sICH reversal algorithm (Table 5).

DWI-FLAIR.clinical_context: Used to select wake-up / unknown-onset patients for IVT (WAKE-UP trial).

perfusion mismatch.clinical_context: Tmax >6s volume minus core volume; used for extended-window selection.

RAPID.clinical_context: Automated perfusion/CTA software used in DAWN and DEFUSE-3.

M3.full_term: M3 segment of middle cerebral artery
M3.clinical_context: Distal MCA branch — EVT not recommended (COR 3 No Benefit).

A1.full_term: A1 segment of anterior cerebral artery

A2.full_term: A2 segment of anterior cerebral artery

P1.full_term: P1 segment of posterior cerebral artery

P2.full_term: P2 segment of posterior cerebral artery

mismatch.clinical_context: Could be DWI-FLAIR (IVT) or perfusion-core (EVT). Parent term.

HT.critical_note: HT in this guideline means hemorrhagic transformation, NOT hypertension. Use HTN for hypertension.
